title: From Me to You: Adachi's Letter
description: Adachi writes her first letter to Shimamura—reflecting on their high school days, the warmth of the gym, and the love that has grown between them.
content:
Dear Shimamura Hougetsu,

I think this is the first time I've ever written you a letter. There haven't been any before, have there? If I had been born in a time when love letters were still common, I probably would have written you a ton of them. I've always felt that I could express myself more clearly and calmly through writing than by speaking.

Back then, I was restless and impatient, so I wonder how much of what I felt ever truly reached you. If I were you, I probably would have thought of my high school self as a weirdo and wouldn't have taken me seriously. That's why you're amazing—and I'm so grateful. You always treated me sincerely, and you still do. Actually, it's not so much that we need to face each other anymore, but that we're now able to stand side by side. And that makes me really happy.

Even now, my skin remembers the warmth of that second floor in the gym. Why did you choose to stay by my side that day, sharing that strange little warmth with me? Sometimes I think back and wonder about it. I wasn't someone interesting to be around—at least I knew that much at the time. What made you go to that hot gym that day? I've always wanted to ask you, so I thought now would be a good time to finally write it down. When you read this letter, I'd really like you to tell me—if you still remember.

Summer always reminds me of our high school days. Maybe it's because that's when everything between us began. I had so many dreams back then, and now it feels like all of them have come true. Every day feels like I'm floating in a soft, hazy dream—but when I touch you, I realize it isn't a dream after all. It's such a simple way to put it, but I really do love it. I love you.

I probably never told you this, but I once had a dream where I kissed you. That was back in high school, I think—that's when my feelings for you first began. I don't really know if the dream came first or the realization did, but that's when I started to truly notice you, when I became aware of you.

Sometimes I still scratch my head thinking about how, when we finally kissed for real, I wasn't nearly as good as I was in that dream. Even as I write this, I can almost feel myself chewing on the memory again. I thought about erasing those lines, but then I realized I'd written them in pen instead of pencil—so I guess I'll just leave them be.

I used to think I was someone who could live alone—someone who had to live alone. But now, I don't think I can live without you. It's not about finding the meaning of life or anything like that. It's just that, for me, life already carries you within it.

Sometimes I wonder how I've changed. Have I become weaker? Or stronger? To find out, I'll keep on living and see where that leads me.

This is the first time I've written and given you a real letter, and I've realized it's actually quite easy—and it makes me feel better. If I'd known that long ago, I might have written you one every morning. It's funny to imagine—you'd probably call me 'The Mailman.'

Also, while writing this, I noticed something. I feel more comfortable writing your name in hiragana.

To me, you'll always be Shimamura. I hope you'll continue being yourself, just as you are, from now on too.

Shimamura, I love you.
